title: Dow Jones Futures Fall, Oil Prices Jump Amid U.S.-Iran Attacks
summary: Dow Jones futures and oil prices will react as the U.S. and Israel continue attacks on Iran, which is vowing revenge for Ayatollah Khamenei.

title: Gold Climbs as Middle East War Drives Demand for Safer Assets
summary: Conflict in the region spread over the weekend after the US and Israel attacked Iran — killing the Islamic Republic’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei — and Tehran responded with waves of missiles at targets in multiple countries.  Wider geopolitical tensions and US President Donald Trump’s upheaval of international relations and trade have underpinned a long-running rally for gold, which has also been supported by elevated central-bank buying and a wider investor shift away from sovereign bonds and currencies.  Bullion tends to benefit when cross-asset market sentiment is “risk-premium first, fundamentals later,” analysts from Franklin Templeton Institute led by Stephen Dover said in a note, recommending “selective gold exposure over broad equity shorts.”

title: Bitcoin Outperforms Equities as Asia Markets Reel From Iran Strikes
summary: Bitcoin holds $66,500 as Asian stocks plunge on Iran strikes. Nikkei drops 2%, oil spikes 13%, and Hormuz closure threatens supply.

title: Oil Prices Soar Amid Escalating Middle East Conflict
summary: Oil prices surged above $80 as U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran disrupted tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz and injected a fresh geopolitical risk premium into global energy markets.

title: Markets begin to react as Trump’s attack on Iran risks hitting American pocketbooks
summary: Oil prices rose in Asia on Monday as investors reacted for the first time to the U.S. and Israel-led conflict in the Middle East. Japan’s Nikkei 225 stock index, which has been on a months-long tear, was down more than 2 percent in early trading. Brent crude, the global oil-price benchmark, rose above $78 per barrel in Tokyo, up almost 8 percent. The move confirmed fears that even a short military operation - or war - in the region would drive oil and U.S. gasoline prices higher. But investors w

title: US futures and Asian shares open lower, oil prices soar as US and Israeli attack Iran
summary: The U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran rattled world markets, with U.S. futures initially falling more than 1% and oil prices soaring, though both moderated as trading picked up early Monday.  The futures for the S&P 500, Dow Jones Industrial Average and Nasdaq composite index had fallen more than 1% earlier, but were down 0.6% by mid-morning in East Asia.  Traders were betting the supply of oil from Iran and elsewhere in the Middle East would slow or grind to a halt.

summary: Oil prices jumped 13% to cross $80 per barrel for the first time since 2024 as traders began rapidly pricing in major disruption to oil flows through the Middle East.

title: Energy In Focus as Global Equity Traders Brace for Iran Impact
summary: “Equity markets are likely to fully shift to oil prices as a primary driver of price action,” said Michael Kantrowitz, chief investment strategist at Piper Sandler & Co. “Equities will be under pressure until oil prices stop rising.”  Meanwhile, riskier growth stocks and economically sensitive groups like industrial and financial stocks could face selling pressure.  Monday should see “volatility and selling in tech and cyclicals, and the reason for that is that, because of the actions that we’ve seen, there will be a significant risk that rising energy prices penalizes growth,” said Matt Gertken, chief geopolitical and US political strategist at BCA Research.
